---
title: "Program"
---

Two days. Mornings are shared — everyone in the same room. Afternoons open into parallel sessions where you choose your own path. Each day closes with something that makes the learning stick.

```{=html}
<div class="day-tabs">
  <button class="day-tab active" onclick="showDay('day1', this)">Day One</button>
  <button class="day-tab" onclick="showDay('day2', this)">Day Two</button>
</div>

<div id="day1" class="day-content active">
```

::: {.card-session}
::: {.time-badge}
8:30 – 9:00
:::
### Arrival & Registration
Pick up your name badge, grab a coffee, and connect with fellow participants before we begin.
:::

::: {.card-session}
::: {.time-badge}
9:00 – 10:30
:::
### Opening: Field Signals — What's Working, What's Broken, What's Next
This session belongs to you. We open the floor to hear directly from participants — what challenges are you navigating in your research, teaching, or practice? What seems to be working in your context, and what isn't? Your input shapes the conversations we have over the next two days.
:::

::: {.card-session}
::: {.time-badge}
10:30 – 11:00
:::
### Morning Break
:::

::: {.card-session}
::: {.time-badge}
11:00 – 12:30
:::
### Breakout Sessions — Part I
Choose one of our parallel sessions and go deep. Each 90-minute session is designed to be hands-on, conversational, and directly applicable to your work. You will stay with the same session for Part II after lunch.

→ [See all sessions](sessions.html)
:::

::: {.card-session}
::: {.time-badge}
12:30 – 2:00
:::
### Lunch
:::

::: {.card-session}
::: {.time-badge}
2:00 – 3:30
:::
### Breakout Sessions — Part II
A second 90-minute block with the same session and speakers. Go deeper, work through questions, and consolidate what you've covered.

→ [See all sessions](sessions.html)
:::

::: {.card-session}
::: {.time-badge}
3:30 – 4:00
:::
### Afternoon Break
:::

::: {.card-session}
::: {.time-badge}
4:00 – 6:00
:::
### Showcase & Connect: Share Your Work, Meet Your Collaborators
Bring a slide, a prototype, a finding, or just an idea you're wrestling with. This is your moment to share what you're working on with a generous, curious audience — followed by drinks and open networking.
:::

```{=html}
</div>

<div id="day2" class="day-content">
```

::: {.card-session}
::: {.time-badge}
8:30 – 9:00
:::
### Arrival & Registration
Welcome back. Coffee is on.
:::

::: {.card-session}
::: {.time-badge}
9:00 – 10:30
:::
### Making Sense of Day One — and Navigating the Funding Landscape
*with Peter Goodyear*

We open Day Two with structured reflection on what surfaced yesterday, then shift to one of the most practical questions in academic life: how do you fund the work that matters? Peter Goodyear joins us to share hard-won insight into the ARC landscape — what reviewers actually look for, how to position your work, and where early-career researchers tend to lose points.
:::

::: {.card-session}
::: {.time-badge}
10:30 – 11:00
:::
### Morning Break
:::

::: {.card-session}
::: {.time-badge}
11:00 – 12:30
:::
### Open Gallery
Yesterday's sessions don't disappear — they come back as a gallery. Each presenter sets up a brief station where you can get a rapid overview of what was covered, ask the questions you didn't get to ask, and make the connections you missed. Move freely, stay as long as you like at each stop.
:::

::: {.card-session}
::: {.time-badge}
12:30 – 2:00
:::
### Lunch
:::

::: {.card-session}
::: {.time-badge}
2:00 – 3:30
:::
### Research Clinic: Design Your Next Move
Bring a half-formed idea — a grant concept, a research design, a school partnership you're trying to shape, or a tool you want to build. Small groups rotate through facilitators who offer structured feedback and help you leave with something concrete: a clearer question, a sharper pitch, or a first draft of a plan.
:::

::: {.card-session}
::: {.time-badge}
3:30 – 4:00
:::
### Afternoon Break
:::

::: {.card-session}
::: {.time-badge}
4:00 – 5:00
:::
### Closing: What We're Building Together
We close not with a summary but with a commitment. What collaborations are forming? What follow-up do we need? Participants share intentions, facilitators map connection points, and we sketch what comes next for this community.
:::

```{=html}
</div>

<script>
function showDay(id, btn) {
  document.querySelectorAll('.day-content').forEach(el => el.classList.remove('active'));
  document.querySelectorAll('.day-tab').forEach(el => el.classList.remove('active'));
  document.getElementById(id).classList.add('active');
  btn.classList.add('active');
}
</script>
```
